title: Eugen Miscoi
content:
People.ContactEmail emiscoi@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Montréal
Known for pragmatic, business‑first counsel, Eugen helps organizations deploy technology securely, manage data responsibly, turn privacy compliance into competitive advantage, and respond decisively to cross‑border incidents with minimal disruption and regulatory exposure.
Eugen Miscoi is a Partner in our Business Law group and a member of the Cyber/Data Group based in Montréal. He advises financial services, technology & e‑commerce, and aviation clients on leveraging digital solutions to create value while meeting Canadian federal and provincial privacy requirements and industry standards. He is often engaged as a trusted breach coach, leading multidisciplinary teams through incident response, regulatory engagement, and post‑event resilience.
Our clients turn to Eugen when they need pragmatic, business‑first guidance that converts complex privacy and cybersecurity requirements into clear decisions, defensible actions, and measurable outcomes:
- Privacy by design, at scale. Translate complex obligations into practical controls, policies, and operating routines tailored to business realities.
- Incident leadership. Coordinate technical, legal, and communications streams for data breaches, ransomware, DDoS, and social engineering—driving fast containment, stakeholder alignment, and defensible reporting.
- Program build‑out. Operationalize enterprise privacy and data governance across lines of business and jurisdictions, aligning risk, compliance, and growth priorities.
- Clear, actionable advice. Frame options and trade‑offs in business terms, enabling executives to move quickly and confidently.
Some of Eugen’s recent experiences include:
- Conducting Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs) for public and private sector organizations, converting requirements into privacy/cybersecurity controls implemented by design and by default.
- Providing strategic and regulatory guidance in the context of over a hundred cybersecurity incidents (e.g., data breaches, social engineering attacks, DDoS, ransomware, etc.), including mandatory notifications and risk mitigation.
- Supporting a major financial institution through remediation of one of the largest data breaches in Canadian history; subsequently helping operationalize its privacy program across business lines and jurisdictions.
- Advising international organizations on Canadian federal and provincial requirements across retail, technology, professional services, manufacturing, and not‑for‑profit sectors.
- Implementing privacy governance frameworks for a large airline and two of Canada’s largest financial institutions.
- Identifying privacy and cybersecurity risks in the context of complex mergers and acquisitions (M&A); informing deal terms (e.g., representations, warranties, covenants), pricing and integration plans; and designs remediation roadmaps that protect value post‑close.
- Implementing incident response policies, playbooks, business continuity plans, and related governance documents.
Eugen was called to the Québec Bar in 2016. He holds an LL.B. from Université de Montréal (2015), an Master’s in International Affairs from Carleton University (2018), and a Master’s of Business Administration (MBA) from Université Laval (2023).
His credentials include the following industry-leading certifications:
- Computing Technology Industry Association (Security+: 2019)
- Internation Association of Privacy Professionals (IAPP): Certified Information Privacy Professional/Canada (CIPP/C: 2020) Certified Information Privacy Manager (CIPM: 2025)
- Certified Information Privacy Professional/Canada (CIPP/C: 2020)
- Certified Information Privacy Manager (CIPM: 2025)
- Certified Data Privacy Solutions Engineer (ISACA CDPSE: 2021)
Eugen serves clients in French, English, Russian, and Romanian.
- Chambers Canada Leading Lawyer: Privacy & Data Protection
Leading Lawyer: Privacy & Data Protection
